name: Spaghetti Bolognese
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a 12-inch noncorrosive skillet over medium heat.
2. Add onion and saute until soft, about 4-5 minutes.
3. Cook the ground beef, breaking it up into fine pieces, just until it loses its raw color, about 6 minutes. Do not brown the meat.
4. Stir carrot and celery into the meat mixture; cook over medium-high heat for 2 minutes.
5. Stir in wine; cook until the wine is evaporated, 4-6 minutes.
6. Stir in milk and nutmeg; reduce heat to medium and cook until the meat is evaporated, about 3-4 minutes.
7. Remove from heat.
8. Press tomatoes and their liquid through a sieve into a bowl; discard seeds.
9. Stir the sieved tomatoes, beef broth, tomato paste, salt, basil, thyme, pepper, and bay leaf into the meat mixture.
10. Heat to boiling; reduce heat to low. Simmer uncovered, stirring frequently, until most of the liquid is evaporated and the sauce is thick, 1-1.5 hours. Remove and discard the bay leaf.
11. Just before serving time, cook perciatelli or spaghetti in a large kettle of boiling salted water just until al dente, 8-18 minutes depending on thickness of pasta. Drain well.
12. Ladle meat sauce over spaghetti in a heated bowl. Sprinkle cheese on top.
